sourcingNotes.poolAssessment: Wikimedia Commons holds ~25 Peninsula-verifiable landscape files that surface on direct queries. Of those, most are CC-BY-SA (§4.1 flags as generally rejected — share-alike contamination risk on a commercial editorial surface). Only one CC-BY 4.0 candidate is specifically marked to Cape Schanck at dawn (Pulpit Rock, BobTanGo 2024), and its EXIF records camera model FC3582 — a DJI Mavic 3 drone body. Per this brief's hard-reject list, drone shots are out, which eliminates the cleanest-licence candidate. This leaves the shortlist with CC-BY-SA candidates that require explicit editor override on the home cover surface, OR a defer-to-Content-Hub decision. Both pathways are surfaced below.
sourcingNotes.hardBlocker: The single most licence-clean Peninsula-verifiable landscape on Commons is BobTanGo's Pulpit Rock 2024 upload, which V1 currently uses. It is a DJI drone shot. The brief hard-rejects drone imagery. This is the first real editorial decision the workflow forces: either relax the drone rule for this one image, relax the SA rule for one of the hand-held alternatives, or defer home-cover-01 until Visit Victoria Content Hub access is live.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY-SA 4.0. Sourcing report §4.1 flags SA as generally rejected on commercial editorial surfaces due to share-alike contamination. Editor override required before this candidate can be approved; the override applies only to the image and its specific derivative, not the surrounding page or site CSS.
candidates.place: Cape Schanck — looking towards Pulpit Rock at dawn
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Hand-held ground-level dawn landscape looking along the basalt towards Pulpit Rock. Strong single focal point, warm early light, no drone provenance, no other figures in frame. Editorially the strongest Peninsula-verifiable landscape on Commons at the required size. Only failure is the SA licence — every other §6 rule passes. Primary candidate conditional on editor accepting SA on this single asset.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY-SA 4.0. Same SA-contamination flag as candidate A. Editor override required.
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Walking-trail perspective, approach composition that fits the 'editorial cover' brief and would feed well into the Explore hub twinning later. Editorial weight is the soft fail — the composition is approach-grade, not cover-grade, and at 2592px wide it sits at the floor of the minimum 2400px requirement with no crop headroom. Secondary option if candidate A's SA override is accepted but A proves unsuitable in review. Not a recommended primary.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY-SA 4.0. Same SA-contamination flag. Editor override required.
candidates.place: Bushrangers Bay — Two Bays walking track view
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: Elevated track-view of the bay below; cool hinterland-to-coast geometry, a characteristic Peninsula-southern-coast frame. Editorially narrower than candidate A but much more specific about the 'approach' brand axis. Works as a cover if the editor wants the home hero to say 'walk to the sea' rather than 'stand at the cliff'. SA contamination same as A and B.

candidates.licenseNotes: CC-BY 4.0 — the only clean-licence non-SA Peninsula landscape found on Commons at editorial resolution.
candidates.place: Cape Schanck — Pulpit Rock from above
candidates.sourcingAgentNote: SOURCING-AGENT-PRE-REJECT. This is the image currently used on the V1 homepage. EXIF examination of the Commons file confirms: camera manufacturer DJI, camera model FC3582 (a Mavic 3 drone body), focal length 6.72mm, f/1.7, ISO 130, exposure 1/3200s — definitively an aerial drone photograph. The home-cover-01 brief explicitly hard-rejects drone shots (§voice and §cliche). I am carrying this candidate in the shortlist as an identified-and-rejected entry so the editor sees I found the cleanest-licence option, understood why it fails this brief, and did not silently pass it through. This entry is not a recommendation. It exists so the rejection is auditable and so the editor can confirm or revoke the drone-shot hard-reject rule at the workflow level (which would re-open a lot of the Commons pool).

editorActionsRequested.question: Accept CC-BY-SA for home-cover-01 only, on the basis that the SA obligation attaches to the image file and its direct derivatives, not to surrounding page code?
editorActionsRequested.defaultIfNoReply: Defer home-cover-01 to Visit Victoria Content Hub unlock.

editorActionsRequested.question: Confirm that drone shots remain a hard reject for home-cover-01, which keeps the BobTanGo Pulpit Rock image out even though it is the cleanest-licence Peninsula landscape available?
Or: defer home-cover-01 to Phase 2 entirely, leave the fog-gradient placeholder in place, and spend the Tier A budget on the hub heroes instead?
